CrossFit Open 24.1 Statistics Recap

Alejandro Reyes

The CrossFit Open is the largest participatory sporting event on Earth, according to the official website, that consist of three workouts spread over three weeks. The first workout of the CrossFit Open 2024 took place last weekend and participants needed to perform the following series of the following exercises with a time cap of 15 minutes,

  • 21 Dumbbell Snatches (arm 1) (50/35 lb)
  • 21 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell
  • 21 Dumbbell Snatches (arm 2) (50/35 lb)
  • 21 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell
  • 15 Dumbbell Snatches (arm 1) (50/35 lb)
  • 15 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell
  • 15 Dumbbell Snatches(arm 2) (50/35 lb)
  • 15 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell
  • 9 Dumbbell Snatches (arm 1) (50/35 lb)
  • 9 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell
  • 9 Dumbbell Snatches (arm 2) (50/35 lb)
  • 9 Lateral Burpees Over Dumbbell

Global distribution of 24.1 scores

An impressive number of 366589 participants submitted a score to the CrossFit Open website, of which 62% participated in the Rx category, 33% participated in the scaled category and 5% participated in the foundations category.

Figure 1: Distribution of completion times for 24.1.

Figure 1 shows the distribution of completion times for each scaling version for both male and female divisions. Across all scaling versions, only 15% completed the workout in less than 10 minutes and 28% could not complete all the repetitions within the 15 minute mark. It was very though workout!

How many seconds away am you from quater-final scores?

Athletes that rank among the top 25% in the Rx category classify to the quarterfinals. Considering the data from 24.1. The table 1 shows the completion times you’d have need to do in 24.1 in order to be within the fastest 25% in your age category.

Table 1: Time cap of the 25% fastest in 24.1 (male)
division Age group 24.1 time
M 16-17 10M 23S
M 18-34 9M 23S
M 35-39 10M 12S
M 40-44 10M 59S
M 45-49 11M 45S
M 50-54 12M 36S
Table 2: Time cap of the 25% fastest in 24.1 (female)
division Age group 24.1 time
F 16-17 10M 55S
F 18-34 10M 14S
F 35-39 10M 46S
F 40-44 11M 20S
F 45-49 11M 53S
F 50-54 12M 36S

For example, I am a 35 year old male and I completed the workout in 13:43, meaning that I’m more than 2 minutes away from being a quarter final athlete!

Comparing scores between CF boxes

I extracted the boxes with the best average scores worldwide that had at least ten athletes in the CF Open leaderboard. I plotted the completion times together with the scores of the CF boxes in Basel, where I live and workout (Figure 2 ).

Figure 2: Distribution of completion times of top 8 world boxes and basel boxes.

It is interesting that in the Rx category, the completion times of my box (CF HelvetiX) is very similar to the world wide distributions for the male division. But for the female division, females in CF HelvetiX are much more stronger than the average and closer to best boxes around the globe.

Conclusion

If you are betting for someone in Basel to classify to the quarter finals, bet for a CF HelvetiX female athlete!
